summary: Broad item decomp/matching PR that added code and header/type recovery across many item translation units, promoted four item TUs to Matching in configure.py, adjusted symbols.txt scope/name metadata for item functions and local data, and produced a bot report showing +0.28% matched code, +0.39% linked code, and 48 new matches.

key_files.role: Promoted itfreeze.c, itpikachutjoltground.c, ithinoarashi.c, and ittincle.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Adjusted item function scopes and converted multiple data/sdata/sdata2 symbols from global address-style names to local @ labels.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: symbols.txt scope and local label hunks

key_files.role: Recovered typed item-vars and attributes fields for Pikachu thunder/jolt, Hinoarashi, and Ness Yoyo.
Recovered typed fields for Kyasarin, Tincle, Oldottosea, and Yoshi Egg Throw item data.
Largest source edit in the PR; bot report lists multiple Ness Yoyo fuzzy-match improvements.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report improvements for itnessyoyo

key_files.role: Large source edit; bot report lists multiple new Tincle matches and configure.py marks it Matching.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report new matches for ittincle

key_files.role: Large source edit; bot report lists Hinoarashi new matches and configure.py marks it Matching.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report new matches for ithinoarashi

key_files.role: Large source edit; bot report lists Freeze new matches and configure.py marks it Matching.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report new matches for itfreeze

key_files.role: Large source edit with bot-reported fuzzy improvements and new matches.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report entries for itflipper

key_files.role: Large source edit with bot-reported exact matches and high fuzzy improvement.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report entries for itkyasarin

key_files.role: Source edit with multiple bot-reported exact matches and configure.py promotion to Matching.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report entries for itpikachutjoltground

what_changed: Changed 24 files: config/GALE01/symbols.txt, configure.py, item headers, and item source/header files for flipper, freeze, hinoarashi, kirby_2F23, kyasarin, nessyoyo, oldottosea, pikachuthunder, pikachutjoltground, tincle, yoshieggthrow, and types.h.
Promoted itfreeze.c, itpikachutjoltground.c, ithinoarashi.c, and ittincle.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.

Added or refined item struct fields in itCharItems.h and itCommonItems.h, replacing pads or untyped integer fields with f32, s32, HSD_GObj*, Item_GObj*, ItemLink*, HSD_JObj*, Vec3, and item-specific attribute structs.
Adjusted symbols.txt metadata by changing some functions from global to local scope and converting many item data/string/float/double symbols from global it_804... names to local @ labels.
Bot report recorded 48 new matches, 18 improvements in unmatched items, +10876 matched-code bytes, +15096 linked-code bytes, +1008 matched-data bytes, and +712 linked-data bytes.

smart_moves.text: The PR paired source decomp work with header/type recovery, allowing item code to use typed fields rather than anonymous padding for several item-specific variable and attribute structs.
The PR updated configure.py only for item TUs that the diff marks as Matching, keeping Matching status explicit at the object list level.
The PR included symbol metadata cleanup for local item data and helper functions alongside decomp edits, which is consistent with matching work that depends on section/scope metadata.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: symbols.txt scope and local @ label changes

decomp_lessons.lesson: For item TU matching work, recovering item-specific vars/attributes fields can be part of making source reviewable and matchable; this PR replaces padding with concrete field types in shared item headers while adding item source implementations.
When a TU reaches matching status, the build configuration must be updated so the object is tracked as Matching rather than NonMatching.

Symbol scope and local-label metadata may need to change with decomp progress; this PR narrows selected item symbols from global to local and converts multiple address-style data symbols to @ local labels.

naming_conventions.convention: Recovered item struct fields in this PR mostly retain offset-style names such as xDD8, x68, x6C, x78, x40, and x44 when semantics are not shown by the slice.
Local data symbols in symbols.txt use @number labels when the PR marks them as local rather than global address-style it_80... names.

Item function names shown in bot report and symbols keep established item-prefix forms such as itTincle_, itFreeze_, itKyasarin_, itPikachutjoltground_, and address-style it_802... names where semantics are not recovered.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use configure.py Matching status changes as a final bookkeeping step after item TU matching work; this PR marks four item source objects as Matching.
Recover header field types for item-vars and attributes to replace pads or generic scalar placeholders, supporting cleaner item source accesses.
Adjust symbols.txt scope/local labels for functions and data when matching work reveals local ownership or local constants.
Use the automated report to identify exact new matches and fuzzy improvements by unit and symbol; the report listed 48 new matches and 18 unmatched-item improvements.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: GALE01 report showed matched code 63.74% (+0.28%, +10876 bytes), linked code 35.40% (+0.39%, +15096 bytes), matched data 38.25% (+0.08%, +1008 bytes), linked data 32.58% (+0.06%, +712 bytes), with 48 new matches and 18 improvements in unmatched items.

follow_up_queries: PR 2420 configure.py NonMatching Matching item objects
decomp-dev bot 48 new matches item TUs

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata, changed file counts, merge state
text_corpus: PR body and bot report
notes: The loaded diff is truncated, so file-specific source tactics inside each edited .c file are only partially visible.
No human review comments are present in the loaded slice; review_feedback is limited to the automated bot report.
The bot report supports match/improvement claims, but the slice does not include build commands, objdiff logs, or manual validation details beyond the report.
The PR body is terse: 'And some new linkages :)', so author intent beyond broad item TU fuzzy matching and linkages is inferred only from changed files and report output.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover item-specific header fields alongside item TU matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2420 shows item matching work coupled with header type recovery: padding and generic fields in itCharItems.h and itCommonItems.h were replaced with concrete f32, s32, pointer, Vec3, and item-specific struct fields while many item source files were edited.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Update MatchingFor when item TUs become matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2420 changed itfreeze.c, itpikachutjoltground.c, ithinoarashi.c, and ittincle.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Symbol metadata cleanup can accompany matching progress
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2420 adjusted symbols.txt scopes and local labels for item functions/data, including global-to-local function scope changes and local @ labels for data/string/float/double constants.

source_update_candidates.title: Item MatchingFor object list includes per-item source files
source_update_candidates.text: In PR 2420, configure.py MatchingFor contains entries for item source objects such as melee/it/items/itfreeze.c, itpikachutjoltground.c, ithinoarashi.c, and ittincle.c, and those entries were changed from NonMatching to Matching.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future agents locate where item TU matching status is tracked; should be curated as a path fact, not inferred globally from intake.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not over-name recovered item fields without semantics
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2420 recovers many item struct field types while keeping offset-style names such as xDD8, x68, x6C, x40, and x44. This may support the existing conservative-naming standard for item type recovery.
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate reinforcement of an existing standard; PR evidence shows the pattern but does not prove a new global rule.

source_update_candidates.title: Treat local data label changes as metadata-backed matching work
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2420 changes multiple symbols.txt data entries from global address-style names to local @ labels while improving matched/link data. This may be useful as an example under data-sections-and-tu-splits, but the truncated slice does not show the full ownership analysis.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report matched/link data improvements
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible example for metadata cleanup, but evidence is insufficient to create a stronger standalone rule.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all changed item TUs fully matched; the title says all item TUs >60% fuzzy match, while configure.py marks only four listed objects as Matching and the bot report lists both new exact matches and fuzzy improvements.
Do not infer human reviewer intent or requested changes; no review comments were loaded.
Do not promote the local @ label changes into a blanket rule that all address-style data symbols should become local labels; this PR only supports the specific metadata edits shown.
Do not claim all source files passed objdiff manually; the available verification evidence is the automated bot report.
